Q6 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

12 sinusoidal message signals each having frequency of 4 kHz are multiplexed by using TDM. Maximum possible quantization error should be atmost 1.1% of peak amplitude of message signal. Number of control bits added per frame are given as 3. Assuming the sampling rate as Nyquist rate, the bit rate of transmission is ___________ kbps.
(Rounded off to one decimal place)

NAT
Correct answer: 696
Solution

696 (690 - 700)

Q7 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

For a minimum hamming distance of “4”, how many errors can be corrected?

NAT
Correct answer: 1
Solution

1 (1 - 1)

Q8 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A discrete memoryless source is generating 12 equiprobable symbols. If a source code scheme with 72% coding efficiency is adopted to encode the symbols, then the average codeword length that can be obtained using the selected source coding scheme is _______ bits/symbol.
(Rounded off to integer value)

NAT
Correct answer: 5
Solution

5 (4.8 - 5.2)

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, c, d)

Correct answer: C

During the course of transmission, White Gaussian noise of zero mean and power spectral density 2 × 10–20 Watt/Hz is added to the signal. In the absence of noise, the amplitude of the received sinusoidal wave for digit 1 or 0 is 1.5 μV. The average probability of symbol error is approximately to

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

Q14 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

An analog signal ) has a probability density function given as

The signal is applied to a quantizer with two quantization levels at ±0.5 Volts. The mean square quantization error is ________. (Rounded off to two decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 0.09
Solution

0.09 (0.06 - 0.12)

Q15 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider a signal detector with an input r = ± A + n where, +A and –A are equally probable signal levels at the input of the detector corresponding to transmitted binary data. If n is a noise variable with variance σ2, which is characterized by the probability density function and the threshold used by the detector is rth = 0, then the minimum SNR (in dB) required at the input of the detector to achieve a maximum error probability of 2.5 × 10–5 will be ___________.
(Rounded off to one decimal place)

NAT
Correct answer: 14.7
Solution

14.7 (12.5 - 17.3)

NAT
Correct answer: 92
Solution

92
